article.body_preview:
Dhaka [Bangladesh], May 16 (ANI): Certain financial institutions in Bangladesh are exploring for Chinese payment channels to settle transactions with Russian banks that were removed from the SWIFT system to overcome the challenges experienced by importers and exporters as a result of Western sanctions following Russia's attack on Ukraine, The Asian Age reported.

The Bangladeshi government is apparently under pressure from some pro-Chinese high-ups to switch to the Chinese currency Yuan for fore

llm_connections.evidence_quote: “Bangladesh can lessen its reliance on the dollar by using the Yuan to pay for around 10 per cent of its import expenses”
llm_connections.scope_justification: This refers to Bangladesh’s import expenses at the country level, matching the feature’s national imports scope.

verification.quote_best_match: Bangladesh can lessen its reliance on the dollar by using the Yuan to pay for around 10 per cent of its import expenses,
verification.judge_reasoning: The article explicitly says, 'According to experts, Bangladesh can lessen its reliance on the dollar by using the Yuan to pay for around 10 per cent of its import expenses.' This directly states the causal effect on import payment reliance at the country level, matching the claimed scope.
